Implemented `b0f4d537` as a new ARC2 task package in [arc2/tasks/task_b0f4d537/__init__.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_b0f4d537/__init__.py), [verifier.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_b0f4d537/verifier.py), [generator.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_b0f4d537/generator.py), and [notes.md](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_b0f4d537/notes.md). The rule is: split on the gray divider, use the 7-wide `4` side to recover stripe columns from its smallest nonempty marker row, then expand the most common non-`5` template row across those gap lengths; rows marked by endpoint-augmented `4` rows are replaced by the expanded non-base template rows in top-down order.

I treated the summary hints as partially useful but corrected them in [notes.md](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_b0f4d537/notes.md), because the crucial detail is the segment expansion induced by the minimal-`4` row, not generic row/column matching. The generator matches the family by explicitly sampling a small alternating template panel, a 7-wide marker panel with top/bottom base rows plus endpoint-marked special rows, and random left/right placement. No new primitive was needed.

Validation passed: `5/5` official examples, `1000/1000` generated examples, and the built artifacts are present at [task.json](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/b0f4d537/task.json), [originals.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/b0f4d537/previews/originals.png), [preview_01.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/b0f4d537/previews/preview_01.png), [preview_02.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/b0f4d537/previews/preview_02.png), and [preview_03.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/b0f4d537/previews/preview_03.png).
